The Buddhist literature Anguttara Nikaya gives a list of sixteen great kingdoms called ‘Sixteen Mahajanapadas’. They were Anga, Magadha, Kasi, Kosala, Vajji, Malla, Chedi, Vatsa, Kuru, Panchala, Matsya, Surasena, Asmaka, Avanti, Gandhara and Kambhoja.
Pair 1 is incorrectly matched: Anga comprised the districts of Bhagalpur and Monghyr in Bihar. It may have extended northwards to the river Kosi and included some parts of the district of Purnea. It was located to the east of Magadha and west of the Rajamahal hills. Champa was the capital of Anga. It was located on the confluence of the rivers Champa and the Ganga. Whereas, Ayodhya was ruled under the Janpadas of Kosala.
Pair 2 is incorrectly matched: The Mahajanapada of Kosala was bounded on the west by the river Gomati. Kosala became a prosperous Kingdom having three big cities under its control i.e., Ayodhya, Saketa and Sravasti. Kosala also annexed the Kingdom of Kashi in its territory. The Kings of Kosala favoured both Brahmanism and Buddhism. In the succeeding phases Kosala proved to be one of the most formidable adversaries of the emergent Magadhan empire.
Pair 3 is correctly matched: Vatsa with its capital at Kausambi was one of the most powerful principalities of the sixth century B.C. Kausambi has been identified with modem Kosam or the river Yamuna near Allahabad. This means that the Vatsas were settled around modern Allahabad. The puranas say that the descendant of the Pandavas, Nichaksu shifted his capital to Kausambi after Hastinapur had been washed away by floods.
Pair 4 is correctly matched: The Matsyas were located in the Jaipur-Bharatpur-Alwar region of Rajasthan. Their capital was at Viratnagara famed as the hiding place of the Pandavas. This region was more suitable for cattle rearing. Some of the most famous edicts of the King Ashoka have been found in Bairat (Jaipur district), the ancient Virat.

q.t:
Which of the following statements are correct with respect to the impact of Iranian invasion of India
during 6th century B.C.?
1. It led to the introduction of Satrap system of administration in North-western India.
2. It led to the introduction of Kharosthi script in North-western India.
3. Mauryan architecture was influenced by the Iranian architecture.
4. The void left due to retreat of Iranian invaders from North-western India were filled immediately by
Chandragupta Maurya.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:

so.t:
Option a is the correct answer.
Option 1 is correct: A satrap was a governor of the provinces of the Achaemenid Empires and it was introduced by Iranians in Indian provinces. Later it was developed by the Sakas ruling in North western and western parts of India. Satraps collected taxes and was the supreme judicial authority and he was also responsible for internal security and raised and maintained an army.
Option 2 is correct: Iranian scribes brought into India the new form of writing known as Kharosthi script. It was written from right to left like the Arabic. Some Ashokan Inscriptions in North-western India were written in this script.
Option 3 is correct: The monument of the Ashokan pillar, especially the bell-shaped capital, is inspired from the Iranian architecture. Iranian influence can also be traced in the preamble of Ashokan edicts and in the words used by it. For instance, the Ashokan scribe used the word lipi for the Iranian word dipi.
Option 4 is incorrect: Alexander defeated the Persian King Darius III in the Battle of Arbela (330 BC) and he filled the void left by the Iranians in North-western India. The Macedonian invasion (Alexander invasion) took place between 326-325 BCE during Nanda’s reign and his retreat from North-western India was filled by Chandragupta Maurya's. Chandragupta Maurya acquired North-western India in 301 BCE by defeating Seleucus who was one of Alexander’s generals. Retreat of Iranians from North-western India was filled by Alexander.

q.t:
With reference to the society in the Age of Buddha, consider the following statements:
1. Buddha’s action of frequently visiting the assembly of Shudras played a major role in improving the social conditions of Shudras.
2. The Dharmasutras enacted during Buddha’s age were silent about the practice of interest-bearing
lending.
3. Women belonging to the Brahmana and Kshatriya Varnas were allowed to receive Upanayana.
Which of the above given statements is/are correct?

so.t:
Option d is the correct answer.
Statement 1 is incorrect: There is no evidence that suggests that Buddha visited the assembly of Shudras. Although they were admitted to the new religious order such as Buddhism and Jainism, their general position remained low. The Varna system placed shudras in the lower rank. All kinds of disabilities were imposed on the shudras.
Statement 2 is incorrect: The Dharmasutras, the Brahmanical law scriptures, condemned interest-bearing lending. They criticized the Vaishyas who lent money to expand trade and commerce. Statement 3 is incorrect: No women irrespective of their varnas could be invested with Upanayana. Upanayana usually marks a boy's acceptance into the life of a spiritual student (brahmacharya) and as a member of the religious community. During this ceremony, the young receive the sacred thread which he wears across his chest from the left shoulder to the waist.

q.t:
With reference to the ‘Harappan Script’, consider the following statements:
1. The Harappan script was written mostly from right to left direction.
2. Most of the inscriptions of the Harappan script are extremely long and have used non-recurring symbols.
3. At present, there is no known bilingual inscription to help decipher the Harappan script.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
Option d is the correct answer. The Harappan Script is the writing system developed by the Indus Valley Civilization and it is the earliest form of writing known in the Indian subcontinent. Examples of Indus writing has been found on seals and seal impressions, pottery, bronze tools, stoneware bangles, bones, shells, ladles, ivory and on small tablets made of steatite, bronze and copper.
Statement 1 is correct: Indus script was generally written from right to left, however, some exceptions wherein the script is written left to right or in a boustrophedon mode are also known. Although the script is undeciphered, the writing direction has been deduced from external evidence, such as instances of the symbols being compressed on the left side as if the writer is running out of space at the end of the row. In the case of seals, which create a mirror image impression on the clay or ceramic on which the seal is affixed, the impression of the seal is read from right to left, as is this case with inscriptions in other cases
Statement 2 is incorrect: The Indus script, also known as the Harappan script, is a corpus of symbols produced by the Indus Valley civilization. Most inscriptions containing these symbols are extremely short, making it difficult to judge whether or not these symbols constituted a writing. system used to record as yet unidentified language(s) of the Indus Valley civilization.Thousands of inscriptions are known from some 60 excavation sites of Harrapan period: most of them are short, the average length is five signs and none of them is longer than 26 signs. Also, the reoccurrence of symbols is generally absent. So, it has become extremely hard to decipher the Harappan language/script.
Statement 3 is correct: The seals used by the Harapan’s carried some form of writing. This script is still a mystery to us because we cannot read it. So far, the other forgotten scripts like those of ancient Egypt could be read again because scholars found some inscriptions written in the forgotten script followed by some of its forms in a known script. We have not discovered any bilingual inscription in Harappa so far. Thus, we do not know what language the Harappan spoke and what they wrote. No bilingual texts have been found so far and its connection with Indian writing systems (e.g., Brahmi, Devanagari and Bengali script) is uncertain. This is the main reason why the Indus Valley Civilization is one of the least known of the important early civilizations of antiquity.

q.t:
With reference to the various sects of ancient India, consider the following statements:
1. Unlike Jainism, Ajivika did not believe in the ‘Doctrine of Determinism”.
2. Unlike Buddhism, Jainism completely denied the existence of soul.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
Option d is the correct answer.
Statement 1 is incorrect: Ajivikas believed in the doctrine of determinism. According to this doctrine, affairs of the entire universe were predetermined including an individual’s fate, to the last detail. The doctrine barred personal efforts to change or accelerate improvement toward one’s spiritual destiny.
Statement 2 is incorrect: Jainism does believe in the soul. It believes that the soul exists forever, even after it has left the body. Each soul is always independent and the soul is responsible for what it does. The soul experiences the consequences of its actions and the soul can become liberated from the cycle of birth and death.

q.t:
Consider the following statements
1. It is the birthplace of eleventh tirthankara Shreyansanath.
2. It is also an important Buddhist site.
3. The Bodhisattva statue in this area mentions Kushana ruler Kanishka.
4. It is located on the confluence of Ganga and Gomti river.
Which of the following regions is described in the above given statements?

so.t:
Option b is the correct answer.
Shreyansanath was the eleventh Jain Tirthankara and he was born to King Vishnu and Queen Vishna at Simhapuri in Sarnath in the Ikshvaku dynasty.
Sarnath is an important Buddhist site, it is here that Buddha gave his first sermon. The deer park in Sarnath is where Gautama Buddha gave his first sermon. In addition, the Dhamek Stupa, a massive stupa is situated in Sarnath.
The inscription on the Bala Bodhisattva statue situated in Sarnath states that Kanishka who ruled from his capital in Mathura had several satraps (provinces) under his commands in order to rule his vast territory.
Sarnath is a place located 10 kilometers north-east of Varanasi near the confluence of the river Ganga and Gomti.

q.t:
With reference to the guilds (Shreni) of ancient India that played a very important role in the country's economy, which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. Every guild was registered with the central authority of the State and the king was the chief administrative authority on them.
2. The wages, rules of work, standards and prices were fixed by the guild.
3. The guild had judicial powers over its own members.
Select the correct answer using the codes given below:

so.t: Option c is the correct answer. Statement 1 is incorrect. Guilds were not under the control of the State and the king was not the chief administrative authority on them. They framed their own rules of membership and professional code of conduct, which even kings were supposed to accept and respect. Statement 2 is correct. The wages, rules of work, standards and prices were fixed by the guild itself. Statement 3 is correct. Guilds could try their members for offence in accordance with their own customs and usages, which came to acquire almost the status of law. A guild member had to abide by both guild and state laws. Guilds had their own laws, based on customs and usage, regarding organization, and had considerable power over guild members.

q.t:
With reference to Gupta administration, consider the following statements:
1. The political authority was highly concentrated in the hands of the king.
2. The district administration was under the charge of ‘Rajukas’,
3. Traders have to pay a commercial tax called ‘Sulka’ to trade in the Gupta Kingdom.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
Option c is the correct answer. The administrative system during the Gupta dynasty was found more or less similar to that of the Mauryan Empire. The dynasty gave more importance to administrative decentralization and helped the rulers to systematically control their territories.
Statement 1 is incorrect. The Gupta administration was decentralized in nature and contained many
feudatories like local kings and smaller chiefs, who ruled over large parts of their empire. These lesser rulers adorned their names with titles like raja and maharaja.
Statement 2 is incorrect. The Mauryan district administration was in the charge of ‘Rajukas’, whose position and functions are similar to today’s district collectors. He was assisted by ‘Yuktas’ or subordinate officials. Whereas the Gupta Empire was divided into divisions called ‘Bhukti’ and each Bhukti was placed under the administrative charge of an ‘Uparika.’ The Bhuktis were divided into districts or Vishayas and each Vishaya was under a Vishayapati.
Statement 3 is correct. The Gupta kings levied a certain commercial tax called Sulka. The organization of traders had to pay this tax, the non-payment of which resulted in cancellation of the right to trade and a fine amounting to eight times of the original Sulka.

q.t:
With reference to the Indo-Greek rule in India, consider the following statements:
1. The Indo-Greeks coins used bilingual inscriptions.
2. They introduced Hellenistic art features that gave rise to Gandhara school of art.
3. The Indo-Greeks introduced the use of caps, helmets, and boots for the first time in India. Which of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
Option a is the correct answer.
After the death of Alexander in 323 BCE, many Greeks came to settle on the north-western borders of India with Bactria (presently in the northern part of Afghanistan, the area lying to the south of the Oxus River and to the north-west of the Hindu Kush mountains).
Statement 1 is correct. The Indo-Greek coins circulated both to the north and south of Hindukush region with several unique features. One of the major features of Indo Greek coins was the use of bilingual inscription on their coins. The Indo-Greek Coins has bilingual inscriptions in Greek and Kharoshthi with the royal portraits on the obverse and images of Greek deities such as Zeus, Apollo, and Athena) on the reverse along with the name and title of the king. They may also include Indian religious symbols on one side of the coins.
Statement 2 is correct. The Indo-Greeks introduced new features of Hellenistic art which gave rise to Gandhara school of art in the north-west frontier of India. It was not purely Greek, but rather it was the outcome of the intermingling and influence of both Indian and central Asian contacts. Example of such art is the Besnagar Pillar inscription of Heliodorus.
Statement 3 is incorrect. The Shaka's and Kushana's who were well known for their military knowledge and equipment, introduced the use of caps, helmets and boots to the warrior classes in India. They also introduced the use of turbans, tunics, trousers, long heavy coats and long boots for all other sections of society also.

q.t:
With reference to social structure during the rule of Satavahana Dynasty, consider the following
statements:
1. Satavahana rulers were the supporters of four-fold varna division of society.
2. Only the kings, and not the Queens were allowed to perform the Vedic sacrifices.
3. Satavahana rulers gave tax free land grants only to brahmanas, and not to Buddhist monks.
Which of the statements given above are incorrect?

so.t:
Option b is the correct answer.
Early Satavahanas were identified with Andhras, they were probably a local Deccan tribe who were gradually Brahamnised.
Statement 1 is correct: The Satavahana kings (such as Gautamiputra Satkarni) claimed to be Brahmans. They considered it their primary duty to uphold the varna system, that is, the four-fold division of social structure determined by varnas.
Statement 2 is incorrect: Kings and queens both performed the Vedic sacrifices. Nanaghat inscription of Queen Nayanika, the widowed queen of Satakarni, mentions that queen performed Vedic sacrifices. They worshipped Vaishnava gods such as Krishna and Vasudeva.
Statement 3 is incorrect: They made liberal sacrificial fees to the Brahmanas, they also promoted Buddhism by granting land to Buddhist monks, especially to Mahayana Buddhists.

q.t:
With reference to the impact of contacts between Central Asia and India on religion, art & Architecture during 200 BC to 300 CE, consider the following statements:
1. It led to the introduction of canal irrigation in India.
2. The use of burnt bricks for flooring and roofing was introduced in India.
3. It led to the introduction of use of the curtains in the Indian theatre.
Which of the statements given above are correct?

so.t:
Option b is the correct answer.
The rulers coming from outside including the central Asian countries between 200 BC to 300 CE known as Shaka-Kushana phase introduced various new elements to trade and agriculture, to administrative
organization, to art and literature, sculpture and pottery, science and technology, and to Indian society.
Statement 1 is incorrect. Tughlags were first to introduce canal irrigation in India. Barani in tarikh-iFirozshahi tells us that Ghiasuddin Tughlaq was the first ruler to undertake construction of canals.
Statement 2 is correct. The rulers of central Asia introduced several material elements in the already existing techniques of Indian architecture. The Saka-Kushana phase is demonstrative of an advance in building activities. They introduced the use of burnt bricks and tiles for both flooring and roofing. The period is also marked by the construction of brick walls. Brick wells may also have been introduced during this period.
Statement 3 is correct. The foreign rulers were patrons of Indian art and drama. The Greeks contributed to the development of the Indian theatre by introducing the use of the curtain, which was called yavanika as an imperative element of theatre that plays an important role in the scenic representation of theatre. The word javanika or yavanika for curtain even finds place in the Natyashastra by Bharata.

q.t:
With reference to the post-Mauryan art and culture, consider the following statements:
1. The Shunga rulers introduced the idea of decorated gateways in Buddhist stupas.
2. During this period, the prayer halls or Chaityas were developed in rock caves.
3. The Lomas Rishi cave is an example of man-made cave built during the post-Mauryan period.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
Option a is the correct answer.
The collapse of the Mauryan rule in 187 BCE paved the way for the emergence of several powers in the Indian subcontinent. The period from the decline of the Mauryas to the rise of the Guptas (2nd century BCE to 3rd century CE) is known in Indian history as the post- Mauryan period.
Statement 1 is correct. Stupas were the burial mounds where the ashes of the dead were kept. In PostMauryan period, the size of stupas became larger and more decorative with the use of Stone in place of wood and brick as used during the Mauryan and Vedic period. The Shunga dynasty introduced the idea of ‘torans’ i.e., a beautifully decorated gateways to the stupas that were intricately carved with figures and
patterns and were evidence of Hellenistic influence. Examples: Bharhut stupa in Madhya Pradesh. Statement 2 is correct. The rock-cut cave architecture emerged during Mauryan Period where it was used as viharas (living quarters) only. In Post-Mauryan period, the rock-cut caves developed Chaityas or the prayer halls along with the viharas.
Statement 3 is incorrect. The Lomas Rishi Cave is a man-made cave in Bihar built during Ashoka Period as a part of sacred architecture of Aajivika Sect. The Udayagiri and Khandagiri caves of Orissa is an example of post-Mauryan caves that are both natural and man-made built by kharavela rulers.

opt.t: It was considered a source of income for the State, a sort of tax paid by the people.
It was totally absent in the Madhya Pradesh and Kathiawar regions of the Gupta Empire.
The forced labourer was entitled to weekly wages.
The eldest son of the labourer was sent as the forced labourer.

q.t: With reference to forced labour (Vishti) in India during the Gupta period, which one of the following statements is correct?

so.t:
Option a is the correct answer.
In the Gupta period Vishti or forced labour became a source of state income and was looked upon as a sort of taxation paid by the people. So, it finds frequent mention along with other taxes in the Gupta inscriptions. The donees were not only provided with land and villages along with right to various taxes, they were also given the right to forced labour. This shows that forced labour was probably more common than in the earlier period.
Most of the inscriptions referring to Vishti come from Madhya Pradesh and Kathiawar regions may suggest this practice was more prevalent in these areas.

q.t:
With reference to Sakas, consider the following statements:
1. They ruled over a large region extending from Afghanistan to Kalinga region of India.
2. They introduced Satrap system of government in India.
3. The first ever lengthy inscription in chaste Sanskrit was issued under a Saka ruler.
Which of the statements given above are correct?

so.t:
Option b is the correct answer.
Indo-Scythians also called Sakas were a group of nomadic Iranian peoples of Scythian origin. They migrated from Central Asia southward into modern day Pakistan and Northwestern India from the middle of the 2nd century BCE to the 4th century CE. The first Saka King in India was Maues or Moga, who established Saka power in Gandhara.
Statement 1 is incorrect: Sakas ruled over the region of northern, western and north-western region.
One branch is believed to have settled in Afghanistan. Another line of the Sakas settled in Punjab with Taxila as the capital. There was another that ruled from Mathura. A fourth branch was that which established itself in Western and Central India from where they continued their rule till about the fourth century A.D. Statement 2 is correct: The Sakas along with the Parthians introduced the Satrap system of government. Under this system the kingdom was divided into provinces each under a military governor called Mahakshatrapa or great Satrap. Governors with lower status were called Kshatrapas or Satraps. These governors issued their own inscriptions and minted their own coins. This is indicative of a more independent status than was otherwise normal in an administrative set-up.
Statement 3 is correct: Rudradaman of Saka dynasty issued first ever lengthy inscription to be written in Sanskrit. All other inscriptions in this country were issued in Prakrit. It is evident that Rudradaman patronized Sanskrit.

opt.t: It is a major ancient Indian text concerning code of conduct for men and women in society.
It is a book containing rules regarding performance, acting, gesture and stage direction.
It deals with the study of Vedic astrology.
It is one of the oldest grammar rule books for Sanskrit language.

q.t: Which of the following is the correct description of ‘Hora Shastra’?

so.t:
Option c is the correct answer.
Option a is incorrect: Manusmriti or laws of manu is a rule book containing role of men and women in society and their interaction in social plane.
Option b is incorrect: Natyasastra by Bharata contains the rules regarding performance, acting, gesture and stage direction.
Option c is correct: Horashastra deals with the study of Vedic astrology. Post-Mauryan Indian astronomy and astrology has a huge Greek influence. The term ‘Hora Shastra’ used for astrology in Sanskrit was derived from the Greek term ‘horoscope’.
Option d is incorrect: Panini’s Ashtadhyayi contains the rules of grammar for Sanskrit language. It dated
around 500 BCE.

q.t:
“He was an Indo-Greek ruler. He administered a large territory in the North-western regions of the
Indian Subcontinent with his capital at Sagala. He was a patron of the Buddhist religion and was converted to Buddhism by Nagasena.”
Which of the following rulers is mentioned in the above paragraph?

so.t: Menander was the Indo-Greek king who was patron of the Buddhist religion. He wrote one of the most important Buddhist works called the Milinda-panha (“The Questions of Milinda”) which was written in Pali. He was converted to Buddhism by Nagasena, who also was mentioned in his work. The kingdom of Menander in the Indian subcontinent extended from the Kabul River valley in the west to the Ravi River in the east and from the Swat River valley (in modern Pakistan) in the north to Arachosia (the Kandahar region) in Afghanistan in the south. He even made his attempts to capture Pataliputrabut was stopped by the army of Vasumitra (the grandson of Pushyamitra Sunga).

opt.t: Capital punishments were totally absent during the Mauryan time.
The Dharmasthiya courts deals with criminal matters only.
The Kantakasodhana courts relied on spies for information about anti-social activities.
Ashoka tried to establish rule of law based on Varna system.

q.t:
Which of the following is correct with respect to the Judicial administration during the Mauryan
empire?

so.t:
Option c is the correct answer.
Justice was administered through courts which were established in all the major towns. Two types of courts were prevalent during this time.
Option a is incorrect: During the Mauryan period, punishments for crimes were usually quite severe. Punishments for offences and crimes ranged from fines to mutilation of limbs, or even capital punishment.
Option b is incorrect: The dharmasthiya courts mostly dealt with civil law relating to marriage, inheritance and other aspects of civil life. The courts were presided over by three judges well-versed in sacred laws and three amatyas (secretaries).
Option c is correct: The main purpose of kantakasodhana courts was to clear the society of anti-social elements and various types of crimes, and it functioned more like the modern police, and relied on a network of spies for information about such anti-social activities.
Option d is incorrect: Pillar Edict IV contains Ashoka’s claim that he had introduced samata in judicial procedure. According to some interpretations this meant that he had established a uniform rule of law, abolishing varna distinctions in punishments.

q.t: Which one of the following books of ancient India has the love story of the son of the founder of Sunga dynasty?

so.t: Option b is the correct answer. The Malavikagnimitra is a Sanskrit play by Kalidas. The play tells the love story of Agnimitra, the Shunga Emperor. He falls in love with the picture of an exiled servant girl named Malavika.

q.t:
With reference to the Ashokan inscriptions, consider the following statements:
1. Minor Rock Edict I mentions that Ashoka was a devotee of Buddhism.
2. Ashokan inscriptions were devoid of Greek and Aramaic script.
3. The Ashokan inscription mentions the region of Kerala as Keralaputho.
4. Ashoka’s edict mentioning the military conquest of Kalinga is the only recorded military expedition of
the Mauryans.
Which of the statements given above are correct?

so.t:
Option b is the correct answer. Statement 1 is correct: The Ashokan inscriptions can be divided into two categories. The smaller group of these inscriptions reveal that the king was a follower of Buddhism and were addressed. to the Buddhist Church or the Sangha. For instance, Minor Rock Edict I mentions that he has been a lay devotee of Buddhism for two and a half years.
Statement 2 is incorrect: The Ashokan edicts are written in the Prakrit language and Brahmi script, and occasionally in the Kharosthi script (in the north-western parts of the subcontinent) There are a few inscriptions in Greek and Aramaic as well. In fact, a bilingual Greek-Aramaic inscription was found at Shar-i-Kuna near Kandahar in south-east Afghanistan and another one in Taxila.
Statement 3 is correct: The second and thirteenth Ashokan stone inscription mentions Kerala as Keralaputho (meaning KeralaPuthra). The second inscription noted that hospitals for humans and animals were established in the neighbouring regions ruled by the Cholas, Pandyas, Satyaputra, Keralaputra and the Thambapanni (Sri Lanka).
Statement 4 is correct: Ashoka’s Major Rock Edict 13 is the only recorded military expedition. of the Mauryans. It mentions Ashoka's military conquest of Kalinga and the disastrous consequences of conquest of territory by war.

q.t:
With reference to the principles of Ashoka’s Dhamma, consider the following statements:
1. It advocated for the abolition of the practise of slavery.
2. It promoted non-violence towards animals.
3. It asked people to show generosity towards both brahmanas and sramanas.
4. It encouraged welfare activities like digging of wells.
Which of the statements given above are correct?

so.t:
Option d is the correct answer.
The principles of Dhamma were so formulated in a manner that is acceptable to people belonging to different communities and followers of any religious sect. Dhamma was not given any formal definition or structure.
Statement 1 is incorrect: Ashoka’s Dhamma did not advocate for the abolition of slavery. His Dhamma asked people to show consideration towards slaves and servants (thus not condemning slavery). Thus, Ashoka’s Dhamma in fact recognised the practise of slavery.
Statement 2 is correct: Dhamma stressed on prohibition of animal sacrifices and festive gatherings and avoiding expensive and meaningless ceremonies and rituals. It also promoted consideration and nonviolence to animals and courtesy to relations and liberality to Brahmins.
Statement 3 is correct: Ashoka’s Dhamma stressed on tolerance of different religious sects in an attempt to create a sense of harmony. Hence it asked people to show generosity towards brahmanas and sramanas.
Statement 4 is correct: The policy of Dhamma also included certain welfare measures like planting of trees, digging of wells, etc

q.t:
With reference to Kautilya’s Arthashastra, consider the following statements:
1. It provides for water cess which varies from one fifth of the produce to one third of the produce.
2. It refers to ‘Samaharta’ as the highest officer in charge of collection of revenue in the Mauryan empire.
3. It mentions “Sita” as land that was gifted to Brahmanas.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
Option a is the correct answer.
Kautilya’s Arthashastra is a theoretical treatise, prescribing how a state ought to be ruled by the King rather than describing an actual state.
Statement 1 is correct: The State’s role in the provision of irrigation was crucial for a strong agricultural set up. The Arthashastra mentions a water cess which amounted to a fifth, a fourth or a third of the produce.
Statement 2 is correct: Arthashastra describes the highest officer in charge of collecting revenue as the Samaharta. Samaharta, Collector of General of revenue was also in charge of exchequer. He was a present-day Minister of finance. He had to supervise all the provinces, fortified towns, mines, forests, trade routes and others, which were the sources of revenue.
Statement 3 is incorrect: Arthashsatra mentions Sita as crown land which was held by the ruler and designated as his own land (svabhumi). These crown lands were cultivated under the supervision of the State, by sharecroppers or tenant cultivators who paid a tax, or even by wage labor. In the Arthashastra, a Sitadhyaksa or superintendent of agriculture is mentioned who probably supervised the cultivation of sita lands.

q.t:
Which of the following can be the correct reasons for the Gupta Period being known as the Golden
age of India?
1. Many great epics like Ramayana and Mahabharata were completed during this period.
2. The art of mural painting flourished during this period.
3. Increased production of Secular literature during this period.
4. The structural temples were built in Northern India for the first time during this period.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:

so.t:
Option d is the correct answer.
The Gupta period in ancient India has been called the Golden Age of India because the period saw many achievements in the field of arts, science, and literature.
Statement 1 is correct: A huge body of religious and secular literature was compiled in this period. The two great epics, the Ramayana and the Mahabharata were finally completed during the Gupta times.
Statement 2 is correct: The mural paintings of this period are found at Ajanta, Bagh, Badami and other places. The finest piece of art of mural painting is Ajanta cave paintings and most of them belong to the Gupta period. They have been marked by their brilliant colours which have not faded even after 14 centuries.
Statement 3 is correct: The growth of secular literature in the Gupta period was significant. Some of the greatest poets lived during this period. For instance, nine jewels or Navratna named as Amarasimha, Dhanvantari, Ghatakarapara, Kalidasa, Kshapanaka, Shanku, Varahamihira, Varuchi, Vetala- Bhatta adorned the court of Chandragupta II. Some of the famous secular works of Kalidasha are Meghadutam, Abhijnanashakuntalam, Raghuvamsha, Kumarasambhava and Ritusamhara.
Statement 4 is correct: It is for the first time in the Gupta period that the temples in the form of structures were constructed in north India. These temples were made in the architectural style known as Nagara. Bhitargaon in Kanpur and the other of stone at Deogarh in Uttar Pradesh are two such temples.

q.t:
With reference to the scholars/litterateurs of ancient India, consider the following statements:
1. Panini is associated with Pushyamitra.
2. Amarasimha is associated with Harshavardhana.
3. Kalidasa is associated with Chandra Gupta-II.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t: Option c is the correct answer. Amarasimha and Kalidasa were one of the Navratnas in the court of Chandra Gupta-II. Panini was a revered scholar belonging to 5th – 6th Century, whereas Pushyamitra Shunga reigned in 2nd BCE.

q.t:
With reference to the contacts between Central Asia and India during 200 BC to 300 CE, consider
the following statements:
1. The trade between Central Asia and India led to the emergence of the old Silk Route.
2. The Indo-Greeks introduced the practice of military governorship in administration.
3. The use of blue glaze on pottery was introduced in India by Sakas and Kushans.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
Option a is the correct answer.
The impact of Central Asian political domination over northern and north western parts of India by rulers coming from outside led to the development of trade, technology, art forms etc.
Statement 1 is correct. The movement of foreigners into India established firmly the basis of regular trade contact between Central Asia and India. Trade contacts with Afghanistan were already existing but now Central Asia also opened up to trade with new routes. One of these routes became famous as the old Silk Route. Traders of different ethnic origins established trading stations and colonies from which the merchants operated. Examples of such places are Kashgar, Yarkand, Khotan, Miran, etc.
Statement 2 is correct. The Indo-Greeks also introduced the practice of military governorship and the governors were called strategos/satraps. They were important for maintaining the power of the rulers over the indigenous people and for blocking invasions from the north west.
Statement 3 is incorrect. The red pottery techniques were widely used in Central Asia, and were replicated in the Indian Subcontinent too. The typical pottery of this period was red ware, both plain and polished with medium to fine fabric. Whereas the use of blue glaze on pottery is an imported technique, first developed by Mongol artisans who combined Chinese glazing technology with Persian decorative arts. This technique travelled east to India with early Turkic conquests in the 14th century. During its infancy, it was used to make tiles to decorate mosques, tombs and palaces in Central Asia

q.t: With reference to the history of India, the terms ‘kulyavapa’ and ‘dronavapa’ denote

so.t:
Option a is the correct answer.
Option a is correct: These are different terms for measuring land during the Gupta period. The Kulyavapa, Dronavapa, Adhavapa are the terms related to land measurements mainly mentioned in the copper plate inscriptions of Gupta era that were found in Bengal. Kulyavapa land must have been equal to around 160 bighas of land. A Dronavapa must be 16-20 bighas and Adhvapa which appears to be the smallest must be around 4-5 bighas of land.

q.t:
With reference to the Mauryan dynasty in India, consider the following pairs:

How many of the pairs given above is/are correctly matched?

so.t:
Option a is the correct answer.
Option 1 is incorrect: Rajukas were judicial officers. They are appointed in both urban and rural areas, whose judicial functions are often combined with assessment of revenue.
Option 2 is correct: Ashoka established a post named Mahamatas who was judicial officer. Mahamatas were provided with judicial responsibilities within the city according to Ashoka’s inscriptions. The edicts urge the mahamatas to be impartial and ensure that people are not imprisoned or punished without sufficient evidence.
Option 3 is incorrect: Arthashastra references to medicine men of various kinds and ordinary physicians (Chikitsakah), midwives (garbhavyadhi) etc.
Option 4 is incorrect: The Yukta was a junior officer entrusted with the task of survey and assessment of
land and revenue collection. They were subordinates of Rajuka and Pradhesika, officials at the district level.

q.t:
Which of the following is the correct chronological order of kings belonging to the Gupta dynasty in
ancient India?
1. Skandagupta
2. Chandragupta I
3. Srigupta
4. Vishnugupta
Select the correct answer using the code given below:

so.t:
Option d is the correct answer.
After the decline of the Kushanas, north India witnessed the rise of the Gupta dynasty. The rulers of this dynasty were able to establish a vast empire that included almost the entire north India.
Option 3: Srigupta (240–280 CE) was the the first ruler of the Gupta Empire who was succeeded by his
son Ghatotkacha (280–319 CE). Both Sri Gupta and Ghatotkacha are mentioned as Maharajas in inscriptions. Option 2: Ghatotkacha was succeeded by his son Chandragupta I (319 to 335 CE) and he is considered to be the first great king of the Gupta Empire. Chandragupta, I held the title of maharajadhiraja (great king over other kings).
Option 1: Skandagupta was considered as the last great king of the Gupta dynasty. He successfully defended an attack by the Huns, but the recurrence of Huns’ invasion strained his empire’s coffers. The Gupta Empire declined after the death of Skanda Gupta in 467 CE.
Option 4: Vishnugupta was the last recognised king of the Gupta line who reigned from 540 to 550 CE.

q.t:
With reference to the coins during Gupta and Mauryan dynasty in ancient India, consider the
following statements:
1. The Guptas issued a larger number of gold coins in comparison to the Mauryans.
2. Unlike Mauryan coins, the coins of Gupta period bore the image of its King.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
Option c is the correct answer.
Coins played an important role in the history of economic development of the country and it is also an indicator of the empire’s economic strength.
Statement 1 is correct: The Guptas issued many gold coins called dinaras but comparatively few silver and copper coins. Mauryans on the other hand issued a large number of silver coins and fewer gold coins.
Statement 2 is correct: The Gupta coins carry the depictions of the activities of the rulers. Gold coins issued by Samudragupta show him playing the veena. But in Mauryan coins kings' image was absent, for instance its punch marked coins carried a symbol of peacock, hill and the crescent.

q.t:
Consider the following statements regarding the ruler Harshvardhana:
1. He was converted to Buddhism by Hiuen Tsang.
2. He abolished the dowry system in his kingdom.
3. According to Fa-Hien there was widespread poverty and famines in his rule.
Which of the above statements are correct?

so.t:
option b is the correct answer.
Statement 1 is correct: In his early life, Harsha was a devout Saiva but later he became an ardent Hinayana Buddhist. Hiuen Tsang converted him to Mahayana Buddhism. Harsha prohibited the use of animal food in his kingdom and punished those who kill any living being.
Statement 2 is incorrect: The dowry system was prevalent in Harsha’s empire. Widow remarriage was not permitted, especially among higher castes. The practice of Dowry and Sati became prevalent.
Statement 3 is incorrect: The famous Chinese pilgrim, Fa-Hien visited India during the reign of Chandragupta II (Much before the reign of Harsha). Out of his nine years of stay in India, he spent six years in the Gupta Empire. He characterizes the Gupta administration as mild and benevolent. He did not write anything related to Harshvardhan’s rule.

q.t:
Consider the following statements regarding Pallavas:
1. The Kanchi Kailasanatha Temple at Kanchipuram was constructed during the reign of Pallavas.
2. Pallava king Narasimhavarman defeated the Chalukyas.
3. Pallava king Mahendravarman I built a five-celled cave temple at Pallavaram.
Which of the above statement are correct?

so.t:
option d is the correct answer.
Statement 1 is correct: The Kanchi Kailasanathar Temple at Kanchipuram are famous temples that were constructed during the reign of Pallavas. Kailasanathar Temple was built by the Pallavas during 650 AD705 AD. Kailasanathar Temple is a Shiva Kanchi, which is one among the three Kanchis in Kancheepuram, the other two being the Vishnu Kanchi and the Jain Kanchi.
Statement 2 is correct: In 642, Narasimhavarman I sent his force to capture and destroy Vatapi, which was the capital of Chalukyas. In this battle, Pulakeshin II loses his life. After the victory, Narasimhavarman I constructed a temple devoted to Maliikarjuna, commemorating his victory in Vatapi.
Statement 3 is correct: Mahendravarman I (Reign: 600 AD – 630 AD) introduced new style of rock-cut temple architecture. He introduced a new technique in the temple architecture. He carved out temples from huge rocks without using bricks, wood and mortar. For example, he built the five-celled cave temple at Pallavaram.

q.t:
Consider the following statements with reference to the Kingdom of Pandyas during Sangam Age?
1. Vanji was the capital city of the kingdom of Pandyas.
2. The Pandyan Kings patronized Tamil Sangams.
3. Karikalan, is considered as the greatest Pandyan King of the Sangam age.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
Option b is the correct answer.
Statement 1 is incorrect: Vanji was the capital of the Cheras (not Pandyas). The Pandyas ruled from the capital city of Madurai. Korkai was their main port, located near the confluence of Thampraparani with the Bay of Bengal. It was famous for pearl fishery and chank diving. Korkai is referred to in the Periplus as Kolkoi.
Statement 2 is correct: The Pandyan kings patronized the Tamil Sangams and facilitated the compilation of the Sangam poems.
Statement 3 is incorrect: Karikalan, son of Ilanjetchenni, is portrayed as the greatest Chola King (not Pandya king) of the Sangam age. Pattinappalai gives a vivid account of his reign. Karikalan’s foremost military achievement was the defeat of the Cheras and Pandyas, supported by as many as eleven Velir chieftains at Venni.

q.t:
Consider the following pairs:

Which of the above pair is/are correct?

so.t:
Option d is the correct answer.
Pair 1 is correct. Ur: The general assembly of the local residents of non-Brahmadeya villages (or Vellanvagai villages). It is believed that members of the assembly were less than ten. Ur is generally a settlement of peasants.
Pair 1 is correct. Sabha: Two inscriptions belonging to the period of Parantaka Ⅰ found at Uttaramerur provide details about the formation and functioning of Sabhas. The Sabha was an assembly of Brahmans/adult male members in the agraharas which enjoyed a large measure of autonomy.
Pair 3 is correct. Nagaram: Nagaram was an assembly of merchants, traders and artisans.

q.t:
The Chinese traveller Yuan Chwang (Hiuen Tsang) who visited India recorded the general conditionsb and culture of India at that time. In this context, which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. The roads and river-routes were completely immune from robbery.
2. As regards punishment for offences, ordeals by fire, water and poison were the instruments for
determining the innocence or guilt of a person.
3. The tradesmen had to pay duties at ferries and barrier stations.
Select the correct answer using the codes given below.

so.t:
Option b is the correct answer.
Yuan Chwang was a Chinese Buddhist monk and traveller who travelled across India for 17 years.
Statement 1 is incorrect: Yuan Chwang has mentioned in his journal that he was robbed and the thugs had decided to sacrifice him, but he was saved by a hurricane.
Statement 2 is correct: The punishment for social offences according to the traveller’s account were to cut
off the nose or an ear or a hand. Minor offences were dealt with fines. And to determine guilt or innocence ordeals by fire water and poison were the instruments which were used.
Statement 3 is correct: Yuan Chwang also mentioned that tax was collected from the tradesman at ferries and barrier stations.

q.t:
The post Gupta period witnessed a rise of a large number of regional kingdoms in ancient India. In this context, consider the following pairs:

How many pair/s is/are correctly matched?

so.t:
Option b is the correct answer.
The Guptas with their seat of power in Uttar Pradesh and Bihar ruled over Northern and Western India from 4th century CE to late 6th century CE with its zenith, from 319 to 467 CE. With its disintegration the Northern India again split up into several kingdoms.
Option 1 is correct: The Maukhari dynasty controlled the vast plains of Ganga-Yamuna doab (modern Uttar Pradesh) from their capital at Kannauj. They earlier served as vassals of the Guptas and later of Harsha's Vardhana dynasty. The Maukharis established their independence during the 6th century A.D.
Around 606 CE, a large area of their empire was reconquered by the later Guptas. According to HieunTsang, the territory may have been lost to King Shashanka of the Gauda Kingdom, who declared independence circa 600 AD.
Option 2 is incorrect: The Gauda Kingdom or Shashankas ruled over the region of Bengal (modern-day West Bengal and Bangladesh). They are the enemies of Pushyabutis of Thanesar. Shahsanka, the Gauda ruler treacherously murdered Rajavardhana, brother of Harshavardhana.
Option 3 is correct: Kamarupa ruled over the region of modern-day Assam from its capital at Pragiyaotisha (modern Gauhati). Bhaskaravarman, king of Kamarupa dynasty made political alliances with Harshavardhana of Thaneswar, against the alliance of the Gauda and East Malwa.
Option 4 is incorrect: The Maitraka dynasty ruled western India (modern Gujarat) from approximately 475 to 776 A.D from their capital at Vallabhi. It was founded by Bhatarka, who was a military governor of Saurashtra under Gupta Empire, who had established himself as the independent around 475 CE.

q.t:
This court poet authored the first formal biography of a King in ancient India. He also authored a
romantic novel in Sanskrit called Kadambari. Who among the following are the king and the author
mentioned here?

so.t:
Option c is the correct answer.
The above-mentioned phrase talks about the Harshacharitra written by Banabhatta. This book gives an account of Harshavardhana’s rise to power. It inspired new genre of literature, the rulers like Ramapala of Bengal and Bihar, Vikramaditya VI of the Western Chalukya dynasty, Kumarapala of the Chalukya dynasty of Gujarat appear as the central characters of the charitakavya like Ramacharita, Vikramankadevacharita and Kumarapalacharita respectively.
Option a is incorrect: Sandhyakar Nandi is a court poet of pala ruler Ramapala. He composed Ramacharitam, a biography of a Pala ruler Ramapala. It describes how forest chiefs were brought into their alliance through lavish gifts by Ramapala. It was written after Harshacharitra.
Option b is incorrect: Bilhana is a court poet of Vikramaditya VI of the Western Chalukya dynasty. He composed Vikramankadevacharita .
Option c is correct: Banabhatta also known as Bana is a court poet of Harshavardhana. He is an author of Harshacharita, a biography of Indian emperor Harsha. It is considered as the first formal biography of a king. It inaugurated a new literary genre in India. Bana also authored a romantic sanskrit novel called Kadambari.
Option d is incorrect: Hemachandra composed the Kumarapala charitra. Kumarapala (1143 – 1172 CE) was an Indian king from the Chaulukya (Solanki) dynasty of Gujarat. He ruled present-day Gujarat.

q.t:
With reference to the ancient king Harshavardhana, consider the following statements:
1. The Buddhist council convened by him at Kannauj resulted in the emergence of Mahayana sect of
Buddhism.
2. Harsha ruled according to the ideals laid down in the Dharmashastras.
Which of the statement(s) given above is/are correct?

so.t:
Option b is the correct answer.
The Chinese pilgrim Hieun Tsang visited India during the reign of Harsha. His accounts were one of the major sources to deduce Harsha’s religious orientation and policy.
Statement 1 is incorrect: The fourth Buddhist Council held under the patronage of Kanishka in Kashmir in 72 AD gave birth to the Mahayana sect of Buddhism. Harshavardhana subscribed to the Mahayana school of thought.
Statement 2 is correct: Harsha ruled according to the ideals laid down in the Dharmashastras. Dharmasastras is a genre of Sanskrit texts on law and conduct and refers to the treatises (sastras) on dharma.

q.t:
With reference to the Imperial Cholas, consider the following statements:
1. They were one of the dynasties ruling South India during the Sangam Age.
2. They extended Indian rule over Ceylon.
3. Their founder was a former feudatory of the Chalukyas.
Which of the statements given above is/ are correct?

so.t:
Option b is the correct answer.
The Imperial Cholas were one of the regional dynasties ruling areas of South India in the 10th century CE. Under them, various illustrious rulers like Aditya Chola, Parantaka I, Rajaraja I, Rajendra Chola, etc contributed immensely to growth of trade, economic prosperity, as well as development of Arts -such as architecture, Temple building (golden period of Dravidian style temples), Bronze sculpting, as well as Literature (Tamil & Sanskrit).
Statement 1 is incorrect: The Imperial Cholas refer to the royal dynasty ruling over South India, especially the region then known as Tondaimandalam (southern portions of modern-day Andhra Pradesh andnorthern regions of modern-day Tamil Nadu) between the 10th and 12th centuries CE. They were a surviving branch of the Cholas, a tribe that used to rule a part of South India during the Sangam Age (300 BCE - 300 CE). The Sangam Age occurred much before the existence of the Imperial Cholas. Hence this statement is incorrect.
Statement 2 is correct: The Imperial Cholas were named so because like the Imperial empires of Europe, which extended their rule over other countries and their people, the Cholas of the 10th century also conquered foreign lands, ruling them, drawing their resources and spreading Indian culture in those lands.
These lands included Ceylon (Sri Lanka), and countries around the Malay Peninsula - like Java, Sumatra, Bali, Cambodia, etc. Hence this statement is correct.
Statement 3 is incorrect: The Imperial Cholas were former feudatories of the Pallavas, not the Chalukyas. Hence this statement is incorrect.
It was the Pallavas, not the Chalukyas who occupied Tondaimandalam. One of the Imperial Chola dynasty’s early rulers, Aditya (son of founder Vijaylaya), defeated Aparajita of the Pallava dynasty, thus ending them and their rule in Tondaimandalam, as he annexed the area under his direct rule.

q.t:
With reference to the Consumer Price Index (CPI), which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. It tracks the average change in the prices paid by consumers for a basket of goods and services over time.
2. It is an indicator of retail inflation in India.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:

so.t:
Context: According to the National Statistical Office (NSO), the retail inflation rate in India surgedto a 14-month high of 6.21% in October, driven by a notable increase in the prices of essential fooditems.
What is Retail Inflation?
• Retail inflation refers to the rate at which the general price level of goods and services boughtby households increases over a specific period.
• In India, retail inflation is measured by the Consumer Price Index (CPI), which tracks thechanges in the prices of a basket of goods and services typically consumed by urban and ruralhouseholds.
Consumer Price Index (CPI):
• CPI is an economic measure that tracks the average change in the prices paid by consumers fora basket of goods and services over time.
• The CPI in India is compiled by the National Statistical Office (NSO) and is categorized into CPIfor urban and rural areas.
These indices are then combined to calculate the CPI (Combined), which gives a
comprehensive overview of inflation for the entire country.

q.t:
With reference to the Blockchain technology, which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. It is a decentralized, distributed ledger system that records transactions across many computers in a way that ensures transparency.
2. It relies on consensus algorithms to validate transactions and maintain network integrity.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:

so.t:
Blockchain technology:
• Blockchain technology is a decentralized, distributed ledger system that records transactionsacross many computers in a way that ensures security and transparency.
• Blockchain networks rely on consensus algorithms to validate transactions and maintainnetwork integrity.
. These mechanisms ensure that only legitimate transactions are added to the chain.

q.t: The Pamban Bridge connects which of the following Indian cities?

so.t:
About New Pamban Bridge:
• The bridge is built by Rail Vikas Nigam Limited and connects the mainland Indian town of Mandapam with the pilgrimage island of Rameswaram in Tamil Nadu.
• The original Pamban Bridge, completed in 1913 and began in 1914. The new Pamban Bridge is a state-of-the-art vertical lift bridge, allowing ships to pass underneath.
• The bridge plays a crucial role in connecting the mainland with the holy island of Rameswaram, a significant pilgrimage site for Hindus.
Pamban Island
• Location: Between India and Sri Lanka, in the Gulf of Mannar.
• Significance: The largest island in Tamil Nadu by area.
• Alternative Name: Also known as Rameswaram Island.
• Geographical Features: Part of a chain that includes Adam’s Bridge (Ram Setu) and Mannar Island.
Connected to the mainland by the Pamban Bridge.

q.t:
Which of the following countries does not border the Baltic Sea?
1. Denmark
2. Poland
3. France
4. Lithuania
Choose the correct answer using the code given below

so.t:
In News: The incidents of undersea cable cuts in the Baltic Sea have raised geopolitical tensions, in the ongoing Ukraine war.
Baltic Sea:
• The largest expanse of brackish water in the world, the semi-enclosed and relatively shallow.
• The Baltic Sea is an arm of the North Atlantic Ocean. It is bound by the Scandinavian Peninsula, the mainland of Northern Europe, Eastern Europe and Central Europe, and the Danish islands.
• The Baltic Sea is artificially linked to the White Sea by the White Sea Canal and to the North Sea by the Kiel Canal.
• Surrounded by countries: Denmark, Germany, Poland, Lithuania, Latvia, Estonia, Russia, Finland and Sweden.
• It includes the Gulf of Bothnia, the Bay of Bothnia, the Gulf of Finland, the Gulf of Riga, and the Bay of Gdańsk.

q.t:
With reference to the Central Industrial Security Force, consider the following statements:
1. The force is led by a Director General (DG) and operates under the Ministry of Home Affairs of India.
2. It is also responsible for guarding Indian borders.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

so.t:
Statement 1 is correct: It is an armed force established under the Central Industrial Security Force Act, 1968.
• The force is led by a Director General (DG) and operates under the Ministry of Home Affairs of India.
Statement 2 is not correct: It is not responsible for guarding Indian borders.
• CISF is providing security to the strategic establishment, including the Department of Space, the Department of Atomic Energy, the Airports, the Delhi Metro, the ports, the historical monuments and the basic areas of the Indian economy such as petroleum and natural gas, electricity, coal, steel and mining.
It also provides counter-terrorism security to various sensitive facilities, as well as private sector operations.
• Presently, CISF is also providing security to the protected persons classified as Z Plus, Z, X, Y.

q.t:
With reference to Exercise Sea Vigil, consider the following statements:
1. Exercise Sea Vigil was conceptualized as a national-level coastal defence initiative after the 26/11 Mumbai terror attacks.
2. The exercise covers the entire Indian coastline and Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ).
3. Exercise Sea Vigil is conducted annually.
Which of the statements given above are correct?

so.t:
Statement 1 is correct: Exercise Sea Vigil was conceptualized after the 26/11 Mumbai terror attacks to bolster coastal defence.
Statement 2 is correct: The exercise covers the entire 11,098 km coastline and the 2.4 million sq. km Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ) and involves the fishing community and coastal population.
Statement 3 is not correct: Exercise Sea Vigil is not conducted annually; it is a periodic exercise coordinated by the Indian Navy and acts as a precursor to TROPEX, which is conducted biennially.

opt.t: All member states of the United Nations are members of ISA.
All State Parties to the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS) are automatically members of ISA.
ISA membership is restricted to countries engaged in deep-sea mining.
Only developing countries are allowed to participate in ISA’s decision-making processes.

q.t: Which of the following statements about the membership of the International Seabed Authority (ISA) is correct?

so.t:
Union Minister of State of Earth Sciences felicitated trainees nominated by the International Seabed Authority.
About International Seabed Authority:
• Establishment: Formed under the 1982 United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS) and the 1994 Agreement for Part XI implementation, the ISA was formally created on November 16, 1994, achieving full operational autonomy by June 1996.
• Headquarters: Located in Kingston, Jamaica.
• Membership: As per Article 156(2) of UNCLOS, all State Parties to UNCLOS are automatically members of the ISA. As of September 2, 2024, ISA has 170 members (169 countries and the European Union).
• Purpose: ISA is responsible for regulating and controlling mineral-related activities in “the Area” (the international seabed) for the benefit of humankind. It ensures environmental protection from deep-seabed mining activities.
• Jurisdiction: The ISA oversees the international seabed area beyond national boundaries, covering the seabed, ocean floor, and subsoil, which constitutes about 50% of the world’s ocean area.

q.t:
With reference to the Codex Alimentarius Commission (CAC), consider the following statements:
1. It is an intergovernmental food standards body, set up in 1963.
2. It was established jointly by the UN’s Food and Agriculture Organisation (FAO) and the World Health Organisation (WHO).
3. India is not a member state of CAC.
How many of the statements given above are correct?

so.t:
Statement 1 and 2 are correct: Codex Alimentarius Commission (CAC) is an intergovernmental food standards body, set up in 1963. The term “Codex Alimentarius” is Latin for “Food Code.”
• It was established jointly by the UN’s Food and Agriculture Organisation (FAO) and the World Health Organisation (WHO), within the framework of the Joint Food Standards Programme.
• Objective: To protect consumer’s health and ensure fair practices in the food trade.
Statement 3 is not correct: Members: Currently, 189 members (188 UN member countries and the European Union).
• The Commission meets in regular session once a year alternating between Geneva and Rome.
• India became a member in 1964.

opt.t: Land the first woman on the moon.
Advance the understanding of Earth’s ocean and atmosphere interactions.
Test the viability of wood as a sustainable material in space exploration.
Explore the Saturn system, including its rings and moons.

q.t: LignoSat, recently seen in the news, aims to

so.t:
Context: Japan has launched LignoSat, the world’s first wooden satellite into space to prove that wood is a space-grade material.
About: LignoSat
• LignoSat, developed by Kyoto University and Sumitomo Forestry Co. is scheduled to orbit the Earth for six months.
• Named after the Latin word for “wood”, LignoSat is made of honoki, using a traditional Japanese crafts technique without screws or glue.
• The satellite will measure how wood endures the extreme environment of space, where temperatures fluctuate from -100 to 100 degrees Celsius every 45 minutes as objects orbit through darkness and sunlight.
• It will also gauge timber’s ability to reduce the impact of space radiation on semiconductors, making it useful for applications such as data centre construction.
